channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: I’m Worried About the Helpless AI Disruptors of the Future
item.description: Venture capitalists are putting young entrepreneurs in incubators again. It sounds like a fun alternative to growing up.

item.title: Sony’s PS5 Price Hikes Prove This Console Generation Is Far From Over. Good.
item.description: The current console generation may last longer than anyone expects.

item.title: With One Million Displaced, Lebanon Turns to Digital Wallets for Aid
item.description: Amid mass displacement and collapsing trust in institutions, digital wallets are becoming critical conduits for aid, connecting diaspora donors directly with communities on the ground.

item.title: Realme GT 7T : ce champion de l’autonomie perd un tiers de son prix
item.description: Le Realme GT 7T est un choix pertinent et solide : son design séduit, ses performances sont convaincantes, et l’autonomie impressionne. Aujourd'hui, sa version 512 Go passe de 699 euros à 482 euros, ce qui en fait un bon smartphone à conseiller.

item.title: Russia Allegedly Swung at VPNs but Accidentally Hit Its Own Banking Sector Instead
item.description: Russia's internet crackdowns sometimes have embarrassing blowback.

item.title: The Hack That Exposed Syria’s Sweeping Security Failures
item.description: When Syrian government accounts were hijacked in March, the breach looked chaotic. But it revealed something more troubling: a state struggling with the most basic layer of cybersecurity.

item.title: The HP iPod? 7 forgotten Apple products you didn’t even know existed
item.description: Not everything Apple has made has turned to tech gold — here are the niche Apple curiosities from the last half-century that fell by the wayside.

item.title: Nos 3 recommandations de voitures électriques d’avril 2026
item.description: La cuvée de ce début d'année sur le marché des voitures électriques est de très bonne facture. Nous avons testé plusieurs références et voici nos trois recommandations

item.title: Using nuclear explosives to bypass the Strait of Hormuz isn’t a novel idea for the U.S.
item.description: The idea for a new canal to move oil from the Middle East had emerged in the mid-1950s. With the world struggling to get oil supplies moving from the Middle East, former House Speaker Newt Gingrich raised eyebrows with a social media post highlighting a radical idea: Use nuclear bombs to cut a new channel along a route that would avoid Iranian threats in the Strait of Hormuz....

item.title: NordVPN VS Proton VPN : quel est le meilleur VPN dans ce duel au sommet ?
item.description: NordVPN et Proton VPN sont deux fournisseurs majeurs de VPN. Tandis que l'un est connu pour être la figure de proue du marché, le second l'est pour son engagement militant dans la protection des données personnelles. Reste encore à savoir lequel des deux est le meilleur.

item.title: On a visité la plus grande usine vélo de France : 100 000 vélos dans un entrepôt XXL et Intersport aux commandes
item.description: Découvrez les coulisses de la MFC Intersport à Machecoul, qui assemble plusieurs centaines de milliers de vélos électriques et musculaires par an, tout en développant les futurs modèles Nakamura.

item.title: Amazfit Helio Strap vs Polar Loop vs Whoop 5.0: Which should you buy?
item.description: We tested three of the best screenless fitness trackers from Whoop, Polar and Amazfit

item.title: Why AI-powered city cameras are sounding new privacy alarms
item.description: These camera systems have created a mass location tracking infrastructure knitted together by artificial intelligence. For decades, cars dictated urban planning in the United States.

item.title: Why tech bros are so worried about AI having bad taste
item.description: As tech giants aura farm for sophistication, critics argue ‘taste-washing’ will lead AI brands to manufacture their own taste in due time. These days, tech bros keep talking about “taste”— the ability to exercise human judgment and determine unique responses while guiding a machine. It’s a rare skillset, as some AI-made media automates content in the form of generic slop. And now tech professionals are the very people worried that technology will rob society of any real taste....
